item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Is this one of those skips that don't make sense unless you read a dozen other skips and/or tales?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like it overall, but this threw me off:

"All entryways have been barricaded from the interior, preventing personnel access."

How do the doors and windows fly open then? Also, the picture shows very clearly that not everything is barricaded. The barricades we do see are on the outside…

and here "During an active phase of SCP-1530, security personnel noticed that the front door of SCP-1530 did not open. It was then noted that the front entryway was unlocked, and Agent Bertke and Agent Montalvo were sent in accordingly." you talk about the front door not being "locked".

Might be a bit nitpicky on my part, as it's such a minor detail, but it did irritate me in a way that I didn't think was intended

item.title: Re: image source
item.content:encoded: Because of this, the second image of the article has been removed.

item.title: image source
item.content:encoded:
House is sourced in an earlier post, to a suitably licensed flickr post.

Mangled cat, however, is not.

https://tineye.com/search/a04da0d94991d41923f9a881477c18783a366ee9/

image is from an AFPBB.com article (published in 2010) about… well, it's in Japanese, but the translated-into-English parts include the phrases "Rogue Taxidermy Show", "Los Angeles", and "La Luz de Jesus Gallery".

I conclude that this means it is not suitably licensed for use on the SCP Wiki.

item.title: Junior Staff Post - WikiWalk Proposal
item.content:encoded:
Edit: author approved of edits via PM

The WikiWalk team is proposing edits to this page. All proposed edits will be in a collapsible below this post, and after 7 days if there are no serious objections to the edits, the feedback received will be taken into account as the edits are made. If you have questions not directly related to the article about this project, please pm a member of the WikiWalk Team.

+ show block
– hide block

This skip mentions a blackboxed SCP that presumably causes teleportation. I feel like a good candidate to fill this slot is SCP-249, since it's a door that teleports things to any number of random locations.

Editing this:

SCP-1530 was discovered during the search for D-2934, after testing with SCP-████.

To this:

SCP-1530 was discovered during the search for D-2934, after testing with SCP-249.

item.title: Hidden link to 1918
item.content:encoded:
Is it just me or is that link to SCP-1918 mid-way in that word meant to be a hidden secret?

If so, well done, I only found it on my second pass of the page!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Dang it I accidentally double voted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: that image is gonna haunt me.

item.title: New Image
item.content:encoded: New image by Nicholas A. Tonell, found here. License is CC 2.0.

item.title: Re: Image removal
item.content:encoded:
Oh wow I completely forgot.
Sorry. ._.

item.title: Re: Image removal
item.content:encoded: You didn't actually edit the page to remove it, so I took it out myself. Here's hoping the original photographer responds!

item.title: Image removal
item.content:encoded: SCP-1530's first image was removed because we could not properly determine whether or not the image was properly licensed or attributed. It may be added once again if/when proper attribution or proper use under CC guidelines is confirmed. Please contact Live2Die or another member of the Image Team if you have questions.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
You…you secret canon contributor, you. Making my shit more notable.

Brilliant work. Upvoted happily.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I legitimately can't finish this because of that second picture so well done I guess. My weak heart actually skipped a beat.

Well I finally got the nerve to work through it (read: covered the pic with my hand as I scrolled by) and I really liked it.
Not only is it quite well written, but it led me to a canon that may very well be my favorite once it reaches a conclusion.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Delicious. Also, requires more upvotes; let me contribute with my +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It was on Visual Records, found by PeppersGhost, but it was originally part of a Japanese art gallery with equally freaky animals.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Late response is late. I can see what you mean by saying it clutters, I may just take out the exploration log. Not the house, though, since it's suppose to give more backstory.

RB effectively committing suicide by proxy and leaving behind some sapient animals. So what?

Not suicide, murdered. But that's cool, can't please everyone. I'm just glad you understood what was going on here.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's good to see some actually gruesome pictures on this site. Where did you find the calico pic?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I don't like this. The house having its own anomalous properties, and the exploration log, both don't add anything to the piece and clutter the concept. Beyond that, this all seems rather pointless. RB effectively committing suicide by proxy and leaving behind some sapient animals. So what?

Also the cat's injuries and abnormalities feel forced just to include the gruesome pic. I don't think I like that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I don't really like the idea of reality benders making SCPs, especially when they need "rituals"

Psssstt… She used an SCP she stole to make them… Though I probably didn't make that clear….my bad

Also, the foundation doesn't chase and kill people. It's secure contain protect, not… Well, you know how that goes.

She thinks they're going to kill her. And she's from an alternate foundation, anyway, with an alternate canon.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I don't know… I don't really like the idea of reality benders making SCPs, especially when they need "rituals". Also, the foundation doesn't chase and kill people. It's secure contain protect, not… Well, you know how that goes. Perhaps direct the comment toward Global Occult Coalition? They like to kill reality benders.

However, I digress. Reality benders probably aren't behind SCPs because frankly, some SCPs are just… Weird. Like SCP-1312. Who could imagine that? Who could know about that? What purpose would that person have for making it?

In short, I dislike the bit about reality benders, but the rest of the article is alright, so upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Prooty good, nice work. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like it even more with the connection stated above.

Letter to the Foundation aside, this has a really classic feel to it. And boy, it's been a while since we've had a nice gruesome image in an article.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The agents tone is a bit wonky, but eh. Over all I liked it.
Very mysterious and weird. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+1

I am entirely unsure what is even going on, and I enjoy the fact.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Pretty good, but there are a few awkwardly worded bits and tensing issues, so no vote. Fix them and I will be happy to +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I actually didn't hate this when it was originally posted, but it has honestly been improved threefold. Very nice work.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thank god you put that final note, I like this so much more now. Good job!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You know what? This is fucking weird and I like it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'll get on to fixing those now.
Edit: and done. Thanks for pointing those errors!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is solid, but

Barking and whining from a dog. Yowling from Felis catus, a domestic cat.

Are you using latin or are you not using latin?

Destruction of other materials, such as glass and wood.

How's destruction sound?

It also lacks a caudal vertebrae (tail).

Also silly.

I could care less

How much less could you care?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It's come a long way. I love the characters, and I really love the narrative. You've put a lot of work into it, and it really shows. :3

Glad I could be of help!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like it. It's really nice. More people should write things like this. It would leave me a very happy soulless.

item.title: Author Post
item.content:encoded:
Thanks to Azzleflux, Vivax, blaqueheart, DrMrTheMinotaur, UglyFlower, TwistedGears, Roget, Crayne, and Fantem.

Yeah, I put a lot of work into this rewrite of another over-eager post of mine. Enjoy!

Oh, and one more thing. This is slightly related to this.

+ Spoiler
- hide

The dead woman is Josephine, after escaping a Foundation site. She was nearly caught, but she had to go to attend to a certain anomaly their. She summoned Salix and Willow who had gone into hiding after the fall of Eden, they got stuck in a looping tussle with the D-Class be causing Josephine's death.

This isn't the actual canonical death of Josephine, only my interpretation.

If you know what Eden is, then you get who the two pets are.
And the link may or may not have to do with this post
